name: Old-Fashioned Filled Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the cookie dough first. Sift the flour, baking powder, and salt together; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, cream the butter and sugars thoroughly. Add the eggs, milk, and vanilla; beat at high speed for 2 minutes.
3. Lower the speed and add the flour mixture gradually. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
4. To prepare the raisin filling, grind the raisins until you have about 1 cup of ground raisins. In a medium saucepan, combine raisins, brown sugar, water, salt, and lemon juice. Bring to a boil and simmer for 3 minutes.
5. Combine the cornstarch mixture (2 tablespoons water with 2 teaspoons cornstarch) in a separate bowl. Use it to thicken the raisin mixture when cool; stir in the chopped nuts.
6. To make the date filling, combine the ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Cook for 3 minutes, breaking the dates with a spoon. Remove from heat, allow to cool, then add the chopped nuts.
7. Roll out the dough on a floured surface until thin (1/8 inch). Cut into 2 3/4-inch rounds with a cookie cutter.
8. Place the rounds on an oiled cookie sheet. Place a heaping teaspoon of filling mixture on each round, top with another round, and crimp the edges closed.
9. Bake at 350°F for exactly 8 minutes. The cookies will be pale yellow. Allow cookies to remain on the cookie sheet until almost cool.
